data.description:

data.title: South Korean shares post best week in more than five years on AI rally
data.description:
Key points:
  • KOSPI falls, foreigners net sellers
  • Korean won weakens against dollar
  • South Korea benchmark bond yield falls
  • For the midday report, please clicknL1N3Z903J

Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** South Korean shares closed lower on Friday on profit-booking ahead of a long holiday weekend, but still posted their biggest weekly gain in more than five years helped by a rally in artificial intelligence-linked stocks.

** The benchmark KOSPI closed down 15.26 points, or 0.28%, at 5,507.01, after rising as much as 1.1% earlier in the day to hit a record high.

** The KOSPI rose 8.2% this week, the biggest since early January 2021, after falling 2.6% last week. The KOSPI climbed for a seventh week in eight.

** South Korean financial markets will be closed from Monday to Wednesday for public holidays.

** South Korea's finance ministry said authorities would still closely monitor the financial markets.

** "The KOSPI's rising momentum has been comparably weaker in February than in January, suggesting potential profit-taking pressure going forward," said Han Ji-young, an analyst at Kiwoom Securities.

** Samsung Electronics rose 1.46% to a record close, after the chipmaker said on Thursday it had started shipping its most advanced HBM4 chips to customers. Peer SK Hynix lost 0.90%.

** Most index heavyweights fell. Battery maker LG Energy Solution slid 3.66%, while Hyundai Motor and sister automaker Kia Corp were down 1.38% and 1.32%, respectively.

** Steelmaker POSCO Holdings shed 1.96%, while drugmaker Samsung BioLogics fell 0.52%.

** Of the total 927 traded issues, 337 shares advanced, while 548 declined.

** Foreigners were net sellers of shares worth 974.1 billion won ($674.21 million).

** The won was quoted at 1,444.9 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.44% lower than its previous close at 1,438.6.

** The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) rose by 0.2 basis point to 3.153%, while the benchmark 10-year yield fell by 7.1 basis points to 3.587%.

($1 = 1,444.8000 won)

data.title: Japan's Sumitomo Forestry to acquire US builder Tri Pointe Homes for about $4.5 bln
data.description:
Japan's Sumitomo Forestry agreed to acquire U.S. homebuilder Tri Pointe Homes for $47.00 per share in cash, valuing the deal at about $4.5 billion, the companies said on Friday.

The offer represents a 29% premium to Tri Pointe Homes' closing price on Thursday, the companies said in a joint statement.

data.title: KlaraBo updates its dividend policy and proposes dividend to the 2026 Annual General Meeting
data.description:
The Board of Directors of KlaraBo Sverige AB has adopted an updated dividend policy to enable recurring dividend distributions. Under the updated policy, up to 25 percent of profit from property management may be distributed to shareholders.

The Board considers the company to have a stable financial foundation and, against this background, has resolved to adopt an updated dividend policy enabling recurring dividends of up to 25 percent of profit from property management. Any proposed dividend shall take into account the company’s financial position, its growth initiatives through acquisitions and investments, and its long-term financial needs.

Since its IPO, the company has had the ambition to become a dividend-paying company over time. The Board now considers that the company’s stable financial position, combined with a predictable outlook for capital requirements, including value-creating renovations, enables this step.

The assessment is that a balanced approach between growth, value enhancement and dividend distribution is, under current market conditions, the right long-term strategy to create shareholder value. Any decisions by the Board regarding share buybacks will continue to be made with consideration to the company’s financial position and, if implemented, will be made separately from the dividend policy.

The Board has resolved to propose a dividend of SEK 0.25 per share to the 2026 Annual General Meeting.

About KlaraBo

We create value by acquiring, developing, refining, and managing residential properties with a long-term focus. Since our founding in 2017, we have expanded across Sweden and continue to grow in areas where people want to live and work. By maintaining and upgrading existing buildings, constructing new ones when conditions are right, and managing our properties in-house, we create value for our tenants, our investors, and the communities in which we operate. KlaraBo is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm and is traded under the ticker KLARA B.

For more information:

Andreas Morfiadakis, CEO KlaraBo

andreas.morfiadakis@klarabo.se

+46 76 133 16 61

This information is information that KlaraBo is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted for publication, through the agency of the contact persons set out above, at 2026-02-13 07:55 CET.

Attachments

KlaraBo updates its dividend policy and proposes dividend to the 2026 Annual General Meeting

data.title: Eurozone Sovereign Yield Spread Tightening Expected to Continue — Market Talk
data.description: The progress in eurozone government bond issuance year-to-date appears conducive to continued compression of yield spreads versus German Bunds, in conjunction with the low-volatility European macroeconomic backdrop, Barclays' rates strategists say in a note. "And perhaps more importantly, potential catalysts for spread widening appear to be lacking," they say. For French government bonds, or OATs, for example, the domestic outlook appears "reasonably benign" for the next few months at least, the strategists say. Year to date, eurozone sovereigns have completed around 18% of their annual government bond issuance targets, according to Barclays' estimate. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: IT selloff sours Indian shares; benchmarks set for weekly drop
data.description:
** Indian shares decline on Friday, with benchmarks losing about 1% each, as IT rout deepens on concerns over artificial intelligence-led disruption and fading hopes of near-term U.S. rate cut

** All 16 major sectors decline

** IT drops as much as 5% during the session, before trimming some of the losses; last down 1.5%

** Ten Nifty IT constituents have lost over $50 billion in market cap in February as Anthropic and Palantir's AI push raises fears of hit to revenues and labour-intensive business model

** Realty falls 2.2% on fears that a slowdown in IT hiring could hurt housing demand in major cities

** Metals drop 3%, dragged by a 6% decline in Hindalco after reporting a fall in December quarter profit

** The sell-off spills over to broader markets with small-caps and mid-caps falling 1.4% and 1.2% respectively

** Session's decline set to drag Nifty , Sensex to weekly losses, as benchmarks surrender earlier gains on optimism over U.S. trade deal

** Other Asian markets (.MIAPJ0000PUS) fall 1.1%, tracking an overnight fall in Wall Street equities as a global tech sell-off intensifies

data.title: Shooting at South Carolina State University leaves two dead, one wounded
data.description:
Two people were killed and one other was wounded in a shooting on Thursday night at a dormitory of South Carolina State University, an historically Black college, prompting a campus lockdown, school officials said in a statement.

The shooting was reported at about 9:15 p.m. local time in an apartment at the Hugine Suites student residential complex, according to the statement, posted on the university's Facebook page.

Few additional official details were immediately available, including whether anyone who perpetrated the shooting was still at large.

"University officials have not yet confirmed the victims' identities or the condition of the wounded person," the statement said.

South Carolina State, whose enrollment numbers more than 3,000 students, is one of two historically Black universities in Orangeburg, South Carolina, a riverfront college town about 40 miles southeast of the state capital, Columbia. The other is Claflin University.

A person answering the phone line for the university public safety office on Thursday night said they were not at liberty to provide any information about the incident.

The university said it had asked the State Law Enforcement Division to investigate the shooting, and that Friday classes had been canceled.

data.title: U.S. Inflation Dynamics Likely to Allow More Fed Rate Cuts This Year — Market Talk
data.description: Inflation in the U.S. looks set to remain above target, but the impulse is negative, paving the way for more rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, Variant Perception says in a note as markets await January CPI data at 1330 GMT. With four meetings chaired by recently nominated Federal Reserve Chair candidate Kevin Warsh before the midterms, "we still expect the Fed to cut rates at least three times this year," its analysts say. Declining inflation breadth and recent downside surprises in inflation reinforce Variant Perception's view that a second wave of inflation will not materialize, the analysts say. "The disinflationary impulse from soft housing and labor markets remains intact." (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: HOOD Extends Losing Streak For Third Day: Cathie Wood Buys $12 Million In Post-Earnings Dip
data.description:
Key points:
  • As per Robinhood’s last closing price of $71.12, Wood invested $12.4 million in the company.
  • On Thursday, ARK bought a total of 174,677 shares of Robinhood, with 123,460 shares purchased by its ARKK ETF.
  • Bitcoin is currently trading 1% lower over the last 24 hours at around $66,300.

Shares of Robinhood are continuing their post-earnings weakness, and Cathie Wood is buying the dip with Ark Investment Management snapping up nearly 175,000 shares of the cryptocurrency firm on Thursday.

The company’s shares closed down nearly 9% on Thursday, marking a third consecutive session of losses, and settled at $71.12, their lowest closing level since June of last year. Robinhood stock reached an all-time high of $152.46 in October but has since lost more than half of its value.

Cathie Wood’s ARK Adds Across ETFs

On Thursday, ARK bought a total of 174,677 shares of Robinhood, with 123,460 shares purchased by its ARKK ETF and 32,435 shares purchased by its ARKW ETF, according to the Ark Invest Tracker on X. Another 18,782 shares were bought by ARKF ETF.

As per Robinhood’s last closing price of $71.12, Wood invested $12.4 million in the company. Wood’s Ark Investment has been buying weakness in Robinhood amid falling Bitcoin prices, driven by several factors, including macroeconomic developments and a weakness in tech stocks.

Bitcoin is currently trading 1% lower over the last 24 hours at around $66,300, well below the all-time high of above $126,000 reached in October.

Truist Securities lowered the firm's price target on Robinhood to $120 from $130 said its views the company's fourth-quarter earnings report as "frustrating." January was" better but not by much," said Truist, adding that it was still positive on the company's longer-term potential but says the shares could see short-term volatility.

HOOD’s Crypto Weakness: Revenue Drops 38%

On Tuesday, Robinhood reported that transaction-based revenue increased 15% to $776 million, driven by growth in options and equities revenue. The company, however, saw weakness in cryptocurrency revenue, which was down 38% to $221 million.

Wall Street has noted that Robinhood should benefit from non-crypto business, including the move into prediction markets. Robinhood said that in the fourth quarter, prediction market volumes more than doubled, with over $12 billion in contracts traded in 2025, the first full year of prediction markets. 

What Is Retail Thinking About HOOD?

Retail sentiment on Robinhood jumped to ‘extremely bullish’ from ‘bearish’ territory a month ago, with message volumes at ‘extremely high’ levels, according to data from Stocktwits.

A user on Stocktwits said that they were “fully expecting a recovery by summer, though.”

https://www.stocktwits.com/nirg18/message/644878767

In the last month alone, the retail message volumes on the stock jumped 738% on Stocktwits and the ticker witnessed more than 3% spike in followers on the platform.

Shares of Robinhood have gained % in the last 12 months.

data.title: Finland's HKFoods reports higher Q4 sales as ready meals segment expands
data.description:
Overview

  • Finland meat producer's Q4 net sales rose 1.2% yr/yr to EUR 270 mln

  • Comparable EBIT for Q4 rose 8.4%, driven by efficiency measures

  • Company proposes EUR 0.08 per share dividend for 2025

Outlook

  • HKFoods expects 2026 comparable EBIT to grow compared to 2025

  • Company sees tight beef availability impacting early 2026

  • HKFoods focuses on poultry and ready meals for strategic growth

Result Drivers

  • RETAIL AND FOOD SERVICE GROWTH - Sales grew in Finnish retail and food service channels, particularly in poultry products and ready meals

  • EFFICIENCY PROGRAM - Comparable EBIT improved due to efficiency program savings and increased production efficiency

  • BEEF PRICE CHALLENGE - Rising beef prices due to shortage partially offset by increased sales prices

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

Q4 Adjusted EBIT continuing operations

EUR 11.20 mln

Q4 Revenue continuing operations

EUR 270 mln

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "strong buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy", no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the food processing peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for HKFoods Oyj is €1.80, about 2.9% above its February 12 closing price of €1.75

  • The stock recently traded at 10 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 12 three months ago
